mainEntity.name: What is the best RO plant capacity for my home in Nepal?

acceptedAnswer.text: For a typical Nepali household, we recommend 12-25 LPH for 3-5 members, 50 LPH for 5-10 members, and 100 LPH for 10+ members. Use our RO Capacity Calculator for precise recommendations based on your daily water consumption.

mainEntity.name: How much does RO plant installation cost in Nepal?

acceptedAnswer.text: Domestic RO purifiers start from NPR 22,200 to NPR 26,700. Commercial RO plants range from NPR 50,000 for 50 LPH to NPR 500,000+ for industrial capacity. Contact us for customized quotes.

mainEntity.name: Do you provide WTP installation services across Nepal?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, we provide Water Treatment Plant (WTP) installation services nationwide across Nepal including Kathmandu, Pokhara, Chitwan, Butwal, Birgunj, Biratnagar, Dharan, and all major cities.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between RO and WTP?

acceptedAnswer.text: RO (Reverse Osmosis) removes dissolved salts and minerals through membrane filtration. WTP (Water Treatment Plant) removes physical impurities, turbidity, and contaminants through multi-stage filtration. Both are often used together for complete water purification.

mainEntity.name: How often should I replace water filter media?

acceptedAnswer.text: We recommend replacing filter media every 1-2 years depending on water quality and usage. Our AMC service includes timely media replacement with genuine KT Premium Media for optimal performance.
